zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon Atte Stone於1450年出生在英國艾可阿爾德利時，他的父親John Atte Stone30歲，母親Elizabeth Empson26歲。他於1480年與Elizabeth Sinclair在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1510年2月10日在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Simon Atte Stone于1450年出生在英国艾可阿爾德利时，他的父亲John Atte Stone30岁，母亲Elizabeth Empson26岁。他于1480年与Elizabeth Sinclair在英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1510年2月10日在英国英格兰艾可格亞特 布羅姆利享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可格亞特 布羅姆利。

nameMeanings.name: Atte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern):
from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Tate (Old English Tāta, from tāt ‘happy’).
usually a variant of   Tait  .

variants.name: Tates
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Tate , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now very rare in Britain. In the US, it is found mainly among African Americans.

variants.name: Tade
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Tate  .

North German and East Frisian: from a Frisian short form of a personal name composed with the ancient Germanic element theud ‘people, race’ (see Tack , compare Thaden ). This surname is very rare in Germany and is apparently not found in East Frisia.
Japanese (pronounced as two syllables): written 蓼 ‘knotweed’. Not a common surname.

variants.name: Tait
meanings: Scottish and northern English: nickname for a cheerful person, from Middle English and Older Scots tait(e) ‘merry, eager, lively’ (Old Norse teitr), also used as a personal name. Compare Tate .

variants.name: Tasso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Giancarlo, Gianpaolo, Lorenzo.  German Dieter.
Italian: topographic name for someone who lived near a prominent yew tree, Italian tasso (from Latin taxus, genitive taxi).
Italian: from the personal name Tas(s)o, derived from the obscure ancient Germanic element tāt, possibly akin to Old High German tāt ‘deed’. Compare English Tate .

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .
English: variant of   Stayner  .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon Atte Stone was born in 1450, in Ardleigh, Essex, England, his father, John Atte Stone, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Empson, was 26. He married Elizabeth Sinclair in 1480, in Great Bromley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 10 February 1510, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Simon Atte Stone est né en 1450, à Ardleigh, Essex, Angleterre, son père, John Atte Stone, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Empson, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Sinclair en 1480, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 10 février 1510, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon Atte Stoneが1450年にイギリスエセックスアルドレイグで生まれた時，父John Atte Stoneは30歳，母Elizabeth Empsonは26歳であった。1480年にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーでElizabeth Sinclairと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1510年2月10日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーで60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon Atte Stone(이)가 1450년 영국 에세스 아들리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Atte Stone(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Empson(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1480년 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 Elizabeth Sinclair(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1510년 2월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon Atte Stone родился в 1450 г. в местности ардлиг, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Atte Stone было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Empson, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Sinclair в 1480 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 10 февраля 1510 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
